definition.name: def-spectral-idempotent
definition.definition: P=theta(2Phi-1) (def-spectral-idempotent, refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:2171-2182): for Phi unital positive almost-idempotent, set S=2Phi-1, R=(S^2)^{-1/2} defined by the binomial series for (1-(1-S^2))^{-1/2}, CONVERGENT since ||S^2-1||=4||Phi^2-Phi||<=4 eta<1 EXACTLY WHEN eta<1/4; then sgn(S)=SR and P=theta(2Phi-1)=(1/2)(1+sgn(S))=(1/2)(1+(2Phi-1)(1-4(Phi-Phi^2))^{-1/2}). WELL-DEFINEDNESS REQUIRES the strict threshold ||Phi^2-Phi||<=eta<1/4 (so the binomial series converges).
